name: Chicken-Liver Pate
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease well and lightly flour five 6-ounce metal juice cans; set aside.
2. In a food processor or blender, whirl half-and-half, butter, onion, and garlic until onion is finely chopped.
3. Add livers, eggs, flour, brandy, salt, sage, thyme, and hot pepper sauce. Whirl until smooth and well blended.
4. Pour mixture into cans to within 1 inch from the top.
5. Place cans in a 10-inch angel-food pan. Add 2 inches of hot water to the pan.
6. Place on the bottom rack of a preheated oven set to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
7. Bake for 1 hour or until a pick inserted into the center comes out clean. Remove cans from the rack and let cool for 30 minutes.
8. With the can opened, cut around the bottoms of the cans and push the bottoms through to unmold pates.
9. Chill completely. Then roll in chopped parsley, wrap in plastic, and again in foil.
10. The pate can be refrigerated up to 1 week or frozen for 3 weeks.
